chapters.blurb: A Tamil empire of temples, bronze, trade and the sea — its rise, its emperors, its genius and its long sunset.

parts.part: I. Rise of the Tiger Throne

episodes.title: Taming the Kaveri: The Grand Anicut
episodes.era: Early Chola root, c. 2nd century CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. Rise of the Tiger Throne
episodes.legend: He chained a wild river and fed a future empire.

episodes.title: Vijayalaya Seizes Thanjavur
episodes.era: Rise of the Imperial Cholas, c. 850 CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. Rise of the Tiger Throne
episodes.legend: From one captured city, he lit the fire of an empire.

episodes.title: The Chola Village Chooses Its Guardians
episodes.era: Chola administration under Parantaka I, c. 919-921 CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. Rise of the Tiger Throne
episodes.legend: A village that carved its rules in stone, not in whispers.

parts.part: II. Rajaraja the Great

episodes.title: Rajaraja Raises Brihadisvara
episodes.era: Imperial Chola, 1003-1010 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. Rajaraja the Great
episodes.legend: He raised a stone mountain for god, art, and empire.

episodes.title: The Shadow of the Great Vimana
episodes.era: Imperial Chola, c. 1003-1010 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. Rajaraja the Great
episodes.legend: He raised a stone mountain that time could not break.

episodes.title: Rajaraja's Sword: Kandalur to Lanka
episodes.era: Imperial Chola conquests, c. 985-1014 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. Rajaraja the Great
episodes.legend: He forged the Chola realm into a true southern empire.

episodes.title: Kundavai, the Chola Princess
episodes.era: Imperial Chola age, c. 980-1010 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. Rajaraja the Great
episodes.legend: She turned royal power into stone, song, and healing hands.

parts.part: III. Rajendra, World-Emperor

episodes.title: Rajendra Brings the Ganges South
episodes.era: Imperial Chola, c. 1012-1044 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Rajendra, World-Emperor
episodes.legend: He brought the sacred Ganga’s water to his southern sea-washed throne.

episodes.title: Rajendra's Fleet to Srivijaya
episodes.era: Imperial Chola naval expedition, c. 1025 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Rajendra, World-Emperor
episodes.legend: The king who hurled India’s war-banner across the eastern seas.

parts.part: IV. Craft, Trade & the Genius of the Age

episodes.figure: Chola bronze artisans and temple patrons
episodes.title: Chola Bronze Artists Make the Divine Dance
episodes.era: Imperial Chola age, 10th-12th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Craft, Trade & the Genius of the Age
episodes.legend: They made metal move like a god in dance.

episodes.figure: Ainnurruvar/Manigramam merchant guilds and Chola-era trade
episodes.title: Tamil Merchant Guilds Sail the Indian Ocean
episodes.era: Chola-era Indian Ocean trade, 11th-13th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Craft, Trade & the Genius of the Age
episodes.legend: They sailed with goods, and ruled the seas with trust.

parts.part: V. The Long Sunset

episodes.title: Kulottunga, the King Who Abolished Tolls
episodes.era: Later Imperial Chola, c. 1070-1122 CE
episodes.chapter_part: V. The Long Sunset
episodes.legend: He won his greatest name not in war, but in mercy.

episodes.title: Kamban Sings the Tamil Ramayana
episodes.era: Later Chola cultural age, c. 12th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: V. The Long Sunset
episodes.legend: He turned Ramayana into the heartbeat of Tamil words.

parts.part: I. The Rise of the Guptas

episodes.title: Chandragupta I and the Licchavi Bride
episodes.era: Founding of the Gupta empire, c. 319-335 CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The Rise of the Guptas
episodes.legend: He turned a wedding into the birth of an empire.

episodes.title: Samudragupta's Hundred Victories
episodes.era: Gupta expansion, c. 335-375 CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The Rise of the Guptas
episodes.legend: He carved his storm of victories into an ancient emperor’s stone.

parts.part: II. The Golden Zenith

episodes.title: Chandragupta II and the Fall of the Shakas
episodes.era: Gupta zenith, c. 375-415 CE (Shaka conquest c. 409 CE)
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Golden Zenith
episodes.legend: He broke the Shaka chains and opened the western seas.

episodes.title: Prabhavatigupta, the Regent Queen
episodes.era: Gupta-Vakataka age, c. 385-450 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Golden Zenith
episodes.legend: A widowed queen who held two empires together.

episodes.title: Faxian's Road Through a Golden Land
episodes.era: Gupta zenith, the traveller's years c. 399-414 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Golden Zenith
episodes.legend: He walked half the world to save the Buddha’s words.

parts.part: III. The Age of Number, Iron and Healing

episodes.title: Aryabhata Turns the Earth
episodes.era: Gupta science, the Aryabhatiya composed 499 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. The Age of Number, Iron and Healing
episodes.legend: He dared to say the Earth itself moves.

episodes.title: The Dot That Became Zero
episodes.era: The Indian journey of zero, Gupta age to 628 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. The Age of Number, Iron and Healing
episodes.legend: He dared to give nothing its own number.

episodes.figure: The Master Smith of the Iron Pillar
episodes.title: The Iron Pillar That Would Not Rust
episodes.era: Gupta metallurgy, c. early 5th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. The Age of Number, Iron and Healing
episodes.legend: He forged a pillar that time and rain could not break.
epithets: Master Smith of the Iron Pillar

episodes.title: Sushruta and the Healer's Knife
episodes.era: The Gupta redaction of Ayurveda, c. 4th-5th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. The Age of Number, Iron and Healing
episodes.legend: He turned the knife of harm into a blade of mercy.

parts.part: IV. Stone, Song and Learning

episodes.title: Nalanda, the Ocean of Learning
episodes.era: Founded under Kumaragupta I, c. 427 CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Stone, Song and Learning
episodes.legend: The king who planted a monastery and grew a world of minds.

episodes.title: Kalidasa and the Cloud Messenger
episodes.era: Classical Sanskrit's peak, c. late 4th-early 5th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Stone, Song and Learning
episodes.legend: His words made an empire of beauty that time could not break.

episodes.figure: The Master Sculptor of the Gupta Age
episodes.title: The Gods Take Shape at Deogarh
episodes.era: The classical moment of Indian art, 5th-early 6th century CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Stone, Song and Learning
episodes.legend: He gave stone a calm face the gods still wear.

parts.part: V. The Long Dusk

episodes.title: Skandagupta Holds Back the Huns
episodes.era: The Gupta twilight, c. 455-467 CE
episodes.chapter_part: V. The Long Dusk
episodes.legend: He stood between the golden age and the Huna storm.

parts.part: I. The Rise of the Peacock Throne

episodes.title: Chandragupta Raises the Peacock Standard
episodes.era: Founding of the Maurya Empire, c. 321-297 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The Rise of the Peacock Throne
episodes.legend: A young challenger from uncertain origins topples the Nandas, gathers Magadha around Pataliputra, and raises the first empire to bind much of ancient India under one throne.

episodes.title: Kautilya's Manual of Spies and Kings
episodes.era: Mauryan statecraft tradition and later Arthashastra compilation
episodes.chapter_part: I. The Rise of the Peacock Throne
episodes.legend: In a world of forts, treasuries, allies and secret agents, the Arthashastra imagines how a ruler might keep order before the strong swallow the weak.

parts.part: II. The Empire Meets the World

episodes.title: Five Hundred Elephants for Seleucus
episodes.era: Seleucid-Mauryan treaty, c. 305-303 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Empire Meets the World
episodes.legend: When Seleucus Nicator marches east to reclaim Alexander's frontier, Chandragupta answers with a treaty of lands, marriage ties, an ambassador, and five hundred elephants that shake the Hellenistic world.

episodes.title: Megasthenes at the Wooden Capital
episodes.era: Greek embassy to Chandragupta's court, c. 300 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Empire Meets the World
episodes.legend: A Greek ambassador walks the long timber walls of Pataliputra, counting towers, gates and river defenses, while trying to describe an India his own world barely understands.

episodes.title: Bindusara's Figs Wine and Philosopher
episodes.era: The transitional Mauryan reign, c. 297-272 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Empire Meets the World
episodes.legend: Between Chandragupta and Ashoka, Bindusara keeps the empire steady and sends west for sweet wine, dried figs and a philosopher the Greeks refuse to export.

parts.part: III. Ashoka's Voice in Stone

episodes.title: Ashoka Appoints the Dhamma Officers
episodes.era: Ashokan dhamma policy, c. 260s-230s BCE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Ashoka's Voice in Stone
episodes.legend: Ashoka sends officials across the roads and provinces, asking an empire of many faiths to practice mercy, restraint, respect and care for the vulnerable.

episodes.title: Prinsep Reads the Forgotten King
episodes.era: Decipherment of Ashoka's edicts, 1837-1838 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Ashoka's Voice in Stone
episodes.legend: After a thousand years of silence, James Prinsep notices tiny repeated letters for 'gift' and unlocks Brahmi, revealing the voice of Devanampiya Piyadasi as Ashoka.

episodes.title: The Four Lions of Sarnath
episodes.era: Ashokan pillar art, c. 250 BCE and modern national adoption in 1947-1950
episodes.chapter_part: III. Ashoka's Voice in Stone
episodes.legend: A pillar raised where the Buddha first taught carries four roaring lions, a shattered wheel, and a polished message that one day becomes India's national emblem.

episodes.title: Mahinda's Bodhi Sapling Voyage
episodes.era: Buddhist missions after the Third Council, c. 250-247 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Ashoka's Voice in Stone
episodes.legend: From Ashoka's court, Mahinda carries teaching to Mihintale while Sanghamitra brings a Bodhi sapling to Anuradhapura, planting a living bridge between India and Sri Lanka.

parts.part: IV. Art in Polished Stone

episodes.title: The Echoing Caves for Ajivikas
episodes.era: Barabar and Nagarjuni cave dedications, c. 259-245 BCE and after
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Art in Polished Stone
episodes.legend: Ashoka cuts mirror-bright chambers into hard granite for the Ajivikas, where every whisper blooms into an echo and stone begins India's rock-cut architecture.

episodes.title: The Didarganj Yakshi's Polished Secret
episodes.era: Early historic sculpture, debated Mauryan or Kushan date
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Art in Polished Stone
episodes.legend: A riverbank reveals a shining yakshi with a fly-whisk, but her mirror polish hides a mystery: is she Mauryan splendor or a later Kushan masterpiece?

parts.part: V. The Long Afterlife

episodes.title: The Parade That Ended the Mauryas
episodes.era: End of the Maurya Empire, c. 185 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: V. The Long Afterlife
episodes.legend: At a military review, the general Pushyamitra kills the last Mauryan king Brihadratha, and the empire of Chandragupta and Ashoka gives way to the Shungas.

episodes.title: Chandragupta's Last Fast at Shravanabelagola
episodes.era: Digambara Jain tradition of Chandragupta's final years, after c. 297 BCE
episodes.chapter_part: V. The Long Afterlife
episodes.legend: In Jain memory, the conqueror lays down his crown, follows Bhadrabahu south, and ends his life in silent fasting on Chandragiri Hill.

parts.part: I. The City of Victory Rises

episodes.figure: Harihara I and Bukka Raya I
episodes.title: Harihara and Bukka Raise a City of Victory
episodes.era: Foundation of Vijayanagara, traditional date 1336 CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The City of Victory Rises
episodes.legend: Two Sangama brothers turn a boulder-ringed river valley into Vijayanagara, a new capital whose origin story blends inscription, politics and sacred memory.

episodes.title: Hampi the Boulder Crowned Capital
episodes.era: Vijayanagara capital at its height, 14th-16th centuries CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The City of Victory Rises
episodes.legend: Among granite hills and the Tungabhadra, Hampi grows into a sacred, royal and agricultural city whose streets astonish visitors from Persia and Portugal.

episodes.figure: Kamalapura Tank and the Bukka Aqueduct
episodes.title: The Tanks That Fed an Empire
episodes.era: Vijayanagara hydraulic works, especially 15th-16th centuries CE
episodes.chapter_part: I. The City of Victory Rises
episodes.legend: In a dry stone landscape, tanks, aqueducts, pipes and channels carry water into fields, palaces and rituals, letting a vast capital survive.

parts.part: II. The Zenith of the Rayas

episodes.title: Krishnadevaraya Takes Raichur
episodes.era: Tuluva dynasty zenith and the Raichur campaign, 1520 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Zenith of the Rayas
episodes.legend: Krishnadevaraya marches on Raichur with nayaka troops, elephants and Portuguese guns, winning a dazzling victory that also hardens Deccan rivalries.

episodes.title: The King Who Wrote Amuktamalyada
episodes.era: Krishnadevaraya's literary court, c. 1509-1529 CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Zenith of the Rayas
episodes.legend: The warrior king becomes a poet, composing Amuktamalyada and gathering Telugu masters whose fame later shines as the Ashtadiggajas.

episodes.title: The Stone Chariot and Singing Pillars
episodes.era: Vijayanagara temple craft, 15th-16th centuries CE
episodes.chapter_part: II. The Zenith of the Rayas
episodes.legend: Granite becomes music, story and motion as Hampi's craftsmen carve the Vittala stone chariot, resonant pillars and Ramayana walls.

parts.part: III. Roads of Witness and Wealth

episodes.figure: Paes, Nunes, Abdur Razzaq and Nicolo de' Conti
episodes.title: Four Travelers Enter the City
episodes.era: Foreign eyewitness accounts, c. 1420-1537 CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Roads of Witness and Wealth
episodes.legend: An Italian, a Persian ambassador and two Portuguese visitors leave dazzling reports of walls, bazaars, armies and festivals in the city of victory.

episodes.title: Diamonds Horses and Pagodas in the Bazaar
episodes.era: Vijayanagara trade networks, 15th-16th centuries CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Roads of Witness and Wealth
episodes.legend: Jewels glitter in temple streets, horses arrive from the Arabian Sea, and gold pagodas carry Hampi into the Indian Ocean economy.

episodes.title: The Nayakas Who Held the Sword
episodes.era: Vijayanagara administration and army, 14th-16th centuries CE
episodes.chapter_part: III. Roads of Witness and Wealth
episodes.legend: The king's power rides through amara-nayakas, military chiefs who collect revenue, raise troops and later reshape South India after the capital falls.

parts.part: IV. Songs, Wit and Shattered Walls

episodes.title: Rakkasa Tangadi Breaks the Capital
episodes.era: Battle of Talikota or Rakkasa-Tangadi, January 1565 CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Songs, Wit and Shattered Walls
episodes.legend: A shifting Deccan coalition captures and beheads Aliya Rama Raya, and Hampi's six-month sack ends the city as imperial capital.

episodes.title: Purandara Dasa Sets the First Notes
episodes.era: Haridasa bhakti and Carnatic music, c. 1484-1564 CE
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Songs, Wit and Shattered Walls
episodes.legend: Purandara Dasa turns devotion into lessons, songs and exercises that become the first steps of Carnatic music for generations.

episodes.title: Tenali Rama Between Poet and Folktale
episodes.era: Krishnadevaraya's court and later folklore, c. 1480-1528 CE and after
episodes.chapter_part: IV. Songs, Wit and Shattered Walls
episodes.legend: Behind the laughing trickster of children's tales stands Tenali Ramakrishna, an attested Telugu poet whose wit later bloomed into folklore.

parts.part: V. Hampi After Empire

episodes.title: Hampi Becomes a World Heritage Memory
episodes.era: Aravidu afterlife and modern heritage, 1570-1646 CE and 1986 onward
episodes.chapter_part: V. Hampi After Empire
episodes.legend: After the capital falls, Aravidu rulers carry the empire onward, while Hampi's ruins later become a UNESCO World Heritage landscape.

periods.label: Ancient India
periods.blurb: Empires, dharma and the classical age

episodes.title: Porus at the River Hydaspes
episodes.era: Punjab polities, 326 BCE
episodes.legend: He lost the battle, not his crown of dignity.

episodes.title: Ashoka's Kalinga Change of Heart
episodes.era: Maurya Empire, c. 261 BCE
episodes.legend: The conqueror who saw the blood, and chose mercy instead.

episodes.title: Abbakka's Refusal at Ullal
episodes.era: Coastal resistance to Portuguese expansion, 16th century
episodes.legend: She faced an empire’s fleet and refused to bow.
epithets: Warrior Queen of the Western Coast

episodes.title: Shivaji's Quiet Escape from Agra
episodes.era: Maratha Swaraj, 1666 CE
episodes.legend: The king who slipped from Agra and lit a free Deccan.

periods.label: The Freedom Struggle
periods.blurb: Revolt, resistance and a free India

episodes.title: Rani Lakshmibai's Stand at Kotah-ki-Serai
episodes.era: Revolt of 1857
episodes.legend: She rode into cannon fire so her people would not kneel.

episodes.title: Mangal Pandey at Barrackpore
episodes.era: Prelude to the Revolt of 1857
episodes.legend: On a dusty parade ground, one sepoy dared to say no.
epithets: Sepoy of the 34th Bengal Native Infantry

episodes.title: Bahadur Shah Zafar and the Red Fort Call
episodes.era: Revolt of 1857
episodes.legend: An old poet-emperor becomes the last fragile banner of freedom.

episodes.title: Kunwar Singh Returns to Jagdispur
episodes.era: Revolt of 1857
episodes.legend: An old lion marches home, sword bright, spirit unbowed.

episodes.title: Tatya Tope's Long March
episodes.era: Revolt of 1857-1859
episodes.legend: The rebel general who turned defeat into a long, burning march.

episodes.title: Tilak Speaks in Court
episodes.era: Early nationalist movement, 1908
episodes.legend: He turned a sedition trial into a cry for swaraj.

episodes.title: Khudiram Bose's Young Resolve
episodes.era: Revolutionary nationalism, 1908
episodes.legend: A teenage boy walked smiling to the gallows for freedom.
epithets: Youngest martyr of Indian freedom struggle

episodes.title: Bagha Jatin by the Buribalam
episodes.era: Revolutionary nationalism during World War I, 1915
episodes.legend: By the Buribalam, he chose duty, not escape.

episodes.figure: Ashfaqulla Khan and Ram Prasad Bismil
episodes.title: The Kakori Promise
episodes.era: Hindustan Republican Association, 1925-1927
episodes.legend: Two friends, one noose, one unbroken dream of freedom.

episodes.title: Lala Lajpat Rai Faces the Simon Commission
episodes.era: Mass politics and constitutional protest, 1928
episodes.legend: He walked unarmed into lathis so a nation could stand tall.

episodes.title: Bhagat Singh Makes the Deaf Hear
episodes.era: Revolutionary nationalism, 1929
episodes.legend: In the Central Legislative Assembly, Bhagat Singh and B. K. Dutt chose a dramatic protest meant to make a message heard rather than to harm bystanders. In court, Singh used clear words to explain why ideas cannot be locked away.
